title: High memory usage in merge JSON type columns
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Question
I have some questions related to MergeTree and JSON-type fields. I'm currently testing the performance differences between JSON fields (with and without hints) and traditional columns. I noticed that in terms of memory usage:
JSON with hint > JSON without hint >> traditional columns.
my test tables:
column tables:
```
CREATE TABLE default.column_table
(
`id` UInt32,
`event_time` DateTime,
s1	Nullable(String),
s2	Nullable(String),
.......
s100	Nullable(String)
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY (event_time,id)
SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192;
```
json column without hints
```
CREATE TABLE default.json_no_hint_table (
`id` UInt32,
`event_time` DateTime,
`data` JSON
) ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY
(id, event_time) SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192
```
json column with 100 hints
```
create table default.json_hint_table
(
`id` UInt32,
`event_time` DateTime,
`data` JSON(`1` String, `10`  .... `99` String, `100` String)
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY (id, event_time)
SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192
```
test result
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yrpm2Z_g5-1MLRBj4bNw6yNwWtYCsToj_9RXX8CVfnw/edit?usp=sharing
I'm trying to understand why JSON fields consume significantly more memory during merges compared to traditional columns in MergeTree. Additionally, I've noticed that using schema hints for JSON fields actually increases memory usage even further, compared to not using any hints.
Is there a reason why JSON fields are so much more memory-intensive during merges? And why does enabling hints seem to make memory usage even worse?
Any insights into how JSON fields are handled internally during merges, especially with and without hints, would be greatly appreciated.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE default.test_json_table (id INTEGER, event_time TIMESTAMP, data CLOB);
INSERT INTO default.test_json_table (id, event_time, data) VALUES (1, '2023-10-01 12:00:00', '{"key1": "value1", "key2": "value2"}');
CREATE TABLE default.test_json_hint_table (id INTEGER, event_time TIMESTAMP, data CLOB);
INSERT INTO default.test_json_hint_table (id, event_time, data) VALUES (2, '2023-10-01 12:05:00', '{"key1": "value3", "key2": "value4"}');
SELECT id, event_time, data FROM default.test_json_table WHERE data LIKE '%"key1":%';
key_bug_features: High memory usage during merges of JSON type columns
